title: refactor(enter): centralize upstream error capture, keep full bodies
author: voodoohop

gist.headline: Bigger nets for bigger bugs
gist.blurb: We've refactored our error handling to capture full upstream error messages instead of truncating them. Now when an external model complains, we get the whole story.
gist.summary: Centralized upstream error handling and increased the maximum error body length to prevent truncation of useful debugging context.
gist.impact: Developers will now see complete upstream error messages in logs, making it easier to debug issues with external model providers.
gist.visual_concept: The Nomnom creature eating a very long, continuous scroll of error logs (paper tape) while the bee mascot watches happily. The scroll shouldn't be cut off, representing the new larger error body limits.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the round tan Nomnom creature happily eating a very long, continuous paper scroll of error logs, while the cute bee mascot in a tiny detective hat watches with a magnifying glass. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
